justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService can take 6s via InventoryService retry, exceeding CartService's 5s timeout. (2) Missing circuit breaker on CartService→OrderService explicitly called out. (3) PaymentService 8s gateway timeout > CartService 5s timeout identified as orphaned call/silent charge risk. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk clearly identified — order created but payment not captured, with detailed explanation of when this occurs. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended with multiple alternatives (saga, reserve pattern, outbox/choreography). (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain-level analysis — every inter-service interaction assessed with specific timeout arithmetic
Identified the circuit breaker acceleration problem: when CB opens on PaymentService, it accelerates orphaned order creation
Concrete fix recommendations with specific timeout values (e.g., PaymentService gateway 8s→4s, InventoryService 3s→2s)
Identified idempotency gap as an additional systemic risk beyond the core rubric requirements
Observability review is comprehensive — correlation IDs, tracing, metrics all flagged as missing
Appropriate escalation routing to MJ for architectural decisions (saga pattern) vs Coach K for observability gaps

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService worst-case 6s exceeds CartService's 5s timeout, explicitly called out. (2) Missing circuit breaker on OrderService identified — clearly flagged in component interactions and resilience assessment. (3) PaymentService 8s timeout > CartService 5s timeout identified as orphaned call risk — marked as 'broken' with detailed explanation. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk identified — order created with inventory reserved but payment not captured, explicitly flagged as 'CRITICAL'. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended — mentioned in multiple places. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain analysis — didn't just identify the four required risks but went deeper with idempotency concerns, inventory reservation leaks, and duplicate submission risks
Concrete timeout arithmetic shown (3s + 3s = 6s > 5s) rather than vague 'timeout issues'
Orphaned payment scenario explained with real-world impact (charges with no matching order)
Practical remediation recommendations with specific numbers (reduce to 2s, increase to >8s)
Strong operational perspective — 3am on-call scenario, runbook gaps, reconciliation needs
Appropriate escalation routing to MJ for architectural issues

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService retry scenario noted via InventoryService 3s+3s, and CartService→PaymentService 5s<8s timeout mismatch explicitly called out. (2) Missing circuit breaker on OrderService clearly identified. (3) PaymentService 8s>CartService 5s orphaned call risk identified as 'CRITICAL TIMEOUT MISMATCH'. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk identified — order created + stock reserved + no payment scenario explicitly described. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended with multiple options (saga, reservation TTL, outbox). (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain analysis — identified all four systemic risks with clear arithmetic and practical impact descriptions
Went beyond rubric requirements: identified idempotency risk on InventoryService retries, missing distributed tracing, and rate limiting gaps
Concrete, actionable recommendations for each failure mode with multiple options
Excellent operational readiness assessment including rollback strategy gaps and on-call documentation needs

Did not explicitly calculate the OrderService chain timeout (3s InventoryService + 3s retry = 6s > CartService 5s) as a standalone finding — the InventoryService retry scenario is noted but the specific 6s>5s math is not spelled out as clearly as the 8s>5s payment timeout
Appropriate escalations routed to MJ for architectural decisions on saga pattern and circuit breaker strategy
